insights.claim: The population-weighted U.S. average for cancer is 7.78%.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted mean across ZCTAs with estimates.

insights.claim: The most-deprived ADI tenth averages 0.3 points lower than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted ADI national-rank decile means; ecological, not causal.

insights.claim: Across ZCTAs, cancer is most associated with adults 65+ (Spearman rho=0.671, n=32,245).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation; place-level association only.
Maximum ZCTA value; ties broken by population.

insights.claim: About 11.8 million people live in ZCTAs at or above the high-burden threshold (11.5%).
insights.method_note: Population in ZCTAs at or above the 90th percentile (11.5%).
